sentence: Subjective:
The patient, a 32-year-old female, presents with a history of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and functional abdominal pain, which was previously diagnosed and treated with Bentyl.
She reports experiencing flares of abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting, and has a history of using Reglan as needed for nausea, which she stopped using in favor of Zofran.
The patient has been instructed to start a low-dose fiber supplement, Citrucel, daily, and to use probiotics to help with gas and bloating.
She has also been advised to avoid spicy foods, fatty foods, and alcohol.
The patient reports that her symptoms worsen after consuming spicy foods, including spicy shrimp, and that she has been experiencing diarrhea and abdominal pain.
She mentions that she has been eating pasta with alfredo sauce, which usually does not bother her, but fried chicken has caused abdominal pain and diarrhea.
The patient has also been drinking tequila and dark liquor, but has noticed that drinking water instead of soda has helped improve her symptoms.
Past medical history:
The patient has a history of IBS, previously diagnosed and treated with Bentyl.
Review of systems:
The patient reports diarrhea, nausea, and abdominal pain.
Current medications:
Citrucel daily; Bentyl up to four times daily for intestinal cramping; Zofran as needed for nausea.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The abdomen is soft, non-tender, with no masses or organomegaly, and normal active bowel sounds.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has IBS and functional abdominal pain, aggravated by high stress, poor eating habits, and poor sleeping habits.
Problem list: IBS; functional abdominal pain; suspected food sensitivities; suspected alcohol sensitivity.
Plan:
I prescribed Citrucel 1 tablet twice daily for regularity.
I advised the patient to use Bentyl 20 mg, 1 tablet before eating and up to four times daily, and to take 1 tablet 30-45 minutes before leaving the house and the second tablet at the restaurant.
I also prescribed Zofran, but advised the patient to reduce greasy and fatty foods and alcohol consumption.
I discussed the possibility of starting a long-term medication, Elavil 25 mg at bedtime, to help manage symptoms and reduce the need for Bentyl and Zofran.
Follow-up:
I have sent the patient's prescriptions to her pharmacy and asked her to follow up in 1 month via email through MyChart to check in on her symptoms and any side effects or issues with Elavil.
